raw_output:
The sky looks blue because of something called light! When the sun shines, it makes all kinds of colors, like red and orange and yellow... but most of them get scattered away by the air we breathe. It's like when you're playing with a big ol' dust storm outside and your toys get covered in dust - that's kinda what's happening to the light!

But blue light is special because it doesn't scatter as much, so it gets to travel straight up into the sky! And since our eyes can see all those colors, we see that blue color shining back at us. That's why the sky looks blue during the day, especially when it's sunny outside!
